Huntington Free Library

[ca.1850-2000.] Printed materials, Publications This collection contains ephemera covering artists, both Native American and non-Native American, whose artwork often focused on Native American subjects. Collection material in English 6378565
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
This collection contains ephemera covering artists, both Native American and non-Native American, whose artwork often focused on Native American subjects. Brochures, auction and exhibition catalogs, correspondence, biographical information, programs from events, photographs, publications, slides, small books, some original sketches. The collection was split between Non-Indian and Indian artists.
NOTES
Only a fraction of the actual name headings are given in the record; see the guide for the complete listing.
Forms part of the Native American Collection. (NAC)
See also the Native American subject vertical file, #9205 and Native American biography vertical file, #9242.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Native American Artists Boxes 1-2
Series II. Additional Artists Boxes 3-4
General
The Collective folder is for single items that contain multiple artists an artist may appear in this folder and in their own alphabetical folder. This is indicated.
General
Native American names may be listed under their Native language or anglicised name, in many cases both forms of the name are listed.
General
Items listed as Removed have most likely been added to other collections.
Cite As:
Native American artist vertical file, #9241. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
